# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that creates releases and publishes packages. On version tags, build the application, create a GitHub release, and publish to npm/PyPI. Make the release process fully automated so developers can just push tags to trigger releases.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent cicd security.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI Release Workflow
on: 
  tag_push:
    types: [created] # or, if you want it on all pushes (default)
  
jobs:
  build-and-publish:
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
     
     steps:
       - name: Checkout code
         uses: actions/checkout@v2.3.1 # If you're using actions/checkout or equivalent, use this instead to automatically fetch your dependencies and run the workflow script against those directories as well (if applicable). Or manually download from https://github.com/{user}/{repo}, then untar or clone them anywhere within the runner
         with:  # If you're using actions/checkout for cloning, it will only store the source code in this directory and not add any files to your project (this is a security feature). Or if manually downloading from https://github.com/{user}/{repo}, then untar or clone them within runner
           repo_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} # Needed because the actions/checkout action uses GITHUB_TOKEN 
         with:  
            persist-credentials: false    # Optional (recommended when you'll be pushing to many different repositories)    
           # Specify other repo details if not using `github.repository` context in your workflow file e.g., pull requests [defaults to the repository from which the action was run] 
         with:  
            github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}    # Needed for getting PR information, if any    
           # Specify a tag when you're using tags in GitHub Actions (e.g., semantic versioning)     
          tag: v${{github.ref}} 
       - name: Build and publish packages  
         run: |   
            echo "Building..."
